title: Talk About Your School
prompt: Describe your school. What time do you go to school? What subjects do you study? What is your favorite subject and why?
exampleAnswer: I go to school at seven o'clock every morning. My school is big and has many classrooms and a large library. I study English, Math, Science, and History. My favorite subject is English because I enjoy learning new words and talking with my classmates.
tips: Speak for at least 20 seconds. Use simple sentences and connect them with 'and', 'but', or 'because'.

title: Your Favorite Hobby
prompt: What is your favorite hobby? How often do you do it? Who do you do it with? Why do you enjoy it?
exampleAnswer: My favorite hobby is playing football. I play football every weekend with my friends in the park. We usually play for two hours. I enjoy it because it is fun and keeps me healthy. Football also helps me make new friends.
tips: Think about WHAT, WHEN, WITH WHO, and WHY. This will help you speak longer.

title: Your Family
prompt: Tell me about your family. How many people are in your family? What do your parents do? What do you like to do together as a family?
exampleAnswer: There are four people in my family: my father, my mother, my younger sister, and me. My father is a teacher and my mother works in a hospital. On weekends, we often go to the park or eat dinner together at home. I love my family very much.
tips: Start with how many people, then describe each person, then say what you do together.

title: Your Favorite Food
prompt: What is your favorite food? Where do you usually eat it? Can you describe how it tastes? Do you know how to make it?
exampleAnswer: My favorite food is fried rice with vegetables and eggs. I usually eat it for lunch at home. It tastes salty, a little spicy, and very delicious. My mother makes the best fried rice. Sometimes I help her cook it on Sunday mornings.
tips: Use describing words like 'delicious', 'spicy', 'sweet', or 'crunchy' to make your answer interesting.

title: Your Morning Routine
prompt: Describe your morning routine. What time do you wake up? What do you do before going to school or work? How long does it take?
exampleAnswer: I wake up at six o'clock every morning. First, I wash my face and brush my teeth. Then I eat breakfast with my family — usually rice or bread. After that, I get dressed and pack my school bag. I leave the house at half past six. My morning routine takes about thirty minutes.
tips: Use time words like 'first', 'then', 'after that', and 'finally' to organise your answer.

title: A School Memory
prompt: Tell me about a happy memory from school. What happened? Who was there? How did you feel? Why is it a special memory for you?
exampleAnswer: One of my happiest school memories was winning a spelling competition in Year 3. I had practised every night for two weeks with my father. When the teacher announced my name as the winner, I felt so proud and excited. All my classmates clapped for me. My teacher gave me a certificate and a book as a prize. I still have that book at home.
tips: Use past tense verbs like 'went', 'felt', 'won', and 'was'. Try to include your feelings.

title: A Sport or Activity You Love
prompt: Describe a sport or physical activity you love. How did you start? What are the rules or how do you do it? What benefits does it give you?
exampleAnswer: I love swimming. I started learning when I was eight years old because my parents wanted me to be safe near water. Now I swim at the community pool every Tuesday and Thursday evening. Swimming is great for the whole body — it strengthens your muscles and improves your breathing. It also helps me relax after a long day at school.
tips: Structure your answer: how you started, what it involves, and why you love it. Aim for 30-40 seconds.

title: A Family Celebration
prompt: Describe a special celebration or event your family has had. What was the occasion? Who came? What did you eat or do? Why was it memorable?
exampleAnswer: Last year, we had a big celebration for my grandfather's seventieth birthday. About thirty relatives came to our house, including aunts, uncles, and cousins from different cities. My mother cooked many traditional dishes, and my uncle brought a large birthday cake with candles. We sang happy birthday, looked at old photo albums, and listened to my grandfather's stories. It was a wonderful evening and I felt very connected to my family.
tips: Use sequencing words: 'first we...', 'then...', 'after the meal...'. Include people's reactions and emotions.

title: Cooking a Favourite Meal
prompt: Describe how to cook a dish you know well. What ingredients do you need? What are the steps? What does it look and taste like when it is ready?
exampleAnswer: One dish I can cook well is scrambled eggs with toast. You need eggs, butter, salt, and bread. First, you crack the eggs into a bowl and mix them with a little salt. Then you melt butter in a pan over low heat. Pour in the eggs and stir gently until they are soft and creamy. While the eggs are cooking, toast the bread. Put the scrambled eggs on top of the toast and serve immediately. It is warm, soft, and satisfying — perfect for breakfast.
tips: Use imperative verbs like 'chop', 'mix', 'pour', 'stir'. Describe texture and taste at the end.

title: A Typical Weekend Day
prompt: Describe a typical Saturday or Sunday for you. What time do you wake up? What activities do you usually do? Is the weekend different from a weekday?
exampleAnswer: On weekends, I usually wake up later than on school days — around eight or nine o'clock. After breakfast, I often do my homework or read for an hour. In the afternoon, I meet friends or visit my grandparents. We sometimes go to the market or a nearby park. In the evening, my family watches a film together or plays board games. Weekends feel more relaxed because there is no school and I can choose how to spend my time.
tips: Compare weekdays and weekends. Use 'usually', 'often', 'sometimes', 'rarely' to vary your language.
